record_of_employment.name_and_address_of.answer:
Lone Star Community Bank
1450 Main Street, Suite 300
Houston, TX 77002
Type of business: Regional community bank (retail and small business banking)

record_of_employment.reason_for_leaving_.answer: Seeking growth into a broader role with more responsibility in credit analysis and relationship management at a larger institution, as well as a shorter commute and better long-term career development opportunities.

record_of_employment.title_job_duties_1.answer: Personal Banker / Senior Teller – Opened and serviced consumer checking, savings, money market, and CD accounts; processed daily teller transactions including cash deposits/withdrawals, cashier’s checks, and wire transfers; assisted customers with online banking enrollment and troubleshooting; balanced cash drawer daily and assisted with branch vault balancing; supported branch loan officers by gathering documentation for consumer loans and HELOC applications; trained and mentored two new tellers on bank policies, compliance, and customer service standards.

record_of_employment.name_and_address_of.answer:
Bayou City Credit Union
7820 Westheimer Road
Houston, TX 77063
Type of business: Member-owned credit union (consumer banking and auto lending)

record_of_employment.reason_for_leaving_.answer: Accepted an opportunity with a community bank that offered broader exposure to commercial banking products, a clearer advancement path, and a more convenient location.

record_of_employment.title_job_duties_2.answer: Member Services Representative – Opened new member accounts, processed loan payments, assisted with auto loan applications, handled routine account maintenance (address changes, card reissues, stop payments), resolved member inquiries regarding fees and statements, and educated members about digital banking tools and financial wellness resources.

record_of_employment.name_and_address_of.answer:
GreenLeaf Market
2205 Richmond Avenue
Houston, TX 77098
Type of business: Grocery store / retail

record_of_employment.reason_for_leaving_.answer: Left to transition from retail into financial services after completing an associate degree in business and securing a position at Bayou City Credit Union.

record_of_employment.title_job_duties_3.answer: Cashier / Customer Service Associate – Operated cash register, handled cash, credit, and debit transactions, balanced till at the end of each shift, assisted customers with returns and product questions, maintained front-end cleanliness, and occasionally trained new cashiers on POS procedures and customer service expectations.

record_of_employment.name_and_address_of.answer:
Houston Youth Center (Nonprofit)
910 Oak Ridge Drive
Houston, TX 77009
Type of business: Nonprofit community youth center

record_of_employment.reason_for_leaving_.answer: Position was part-time and grant-funded while attending college; transitioned to full-time work in retail with more stable hours and income.

record_of_employment.title_job_duties_4.answer: Administrative Assistant (Part-Time) – Answered phones, greeted visitors, maintained sign-in logs, assisted with basic bookkeeping tasks such as recording donations and preparing deposit slips, updated participant records, helped coordinate after-school program schedules, and supported staff with filing and data entry.

authorization_and_additional_information.answer: Over nine years of hands-on experience in customer-facing financial and retail roles, including six years in banking and credit union environments with direct responsibility for handling cash, opening accounts, processing wire transfers, and explaining financial products to a diverse customer base. I am very comfortable working with detailed procedures, following regulatory requirements, and maintaining high accuracy under time pressure, as reflected in consistently clean audit results and a strong track record of error-free cash balancing.

Strong communication and relationship-building skills developed by working daily with customers who often arrived frustrated, confused, or anxious about their finances. I make a point of listening carefully, asking clarifying questions, and then walking people through their options step-by-step without using jargon. For example, at Lone Star Community Bank I regularly helped older customers enroll in online banking, patiently demonstrating how to check balances, set up alerts, and avoid overdraft fees, which significantly reduced their in-branch visits and complaint calls. I am bilingual in English and Spanish, which has allowed me to serve Spanish-speaking customers more effectively and ensure they fully understand account terms and conditions before signing.

Detail-oriented and highly organized with strong technical aptitude: proficient with standard banking platforms (FIS Core, Jack Henry Teller, and similar systems), Microsoft Excel for basic tracking and reconciliation, and digital communication tools. I am comfortable learning new software quickly and documenting processes so that others can follow them. I have also taken initiative in previous roles to create simple checklists and quick-reference guides for new team members on topics such as CIP/KYC documentation, common fraud red flags, and steps for processing wire transfers, which reduced training time and helped improve consistency and compliance across the branch.
